---
title: "How the MSE work fits together"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">How the Assessment, MSE, and CMP Documents Fit Together</div>

This page condenses SCW17/Paper-04. It shows where this documentation site fits
within the jack mackerel assessment and MSE work.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  J["JJM assessment configurations"] --> C["Condition operating models"]
  C --> O["Operating models with observation and implementation uncertainty"]
  O --> M["Test CMPs in jmMSE"]
  M --> P["Performance metrics and diagnostics"]
  P --> D["CMP comparison and decision"]
  D --> S["Approved written CMP definition in jmMSE26"]
  S --> Y["Apply the CMP when new annual data arrive"]
  Y --> V["Save the data, calculations, and advice"]
```

| Layer | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| JJM | Assessment-conditioned stock and fishery dynamics |
| `jjmR` / `FLjjm` | Transfer JJM assessment results into the MSE framework |
| FLR / `mse` / `msemodules` | General software used to run projections and management procedures |
| `jmMSE` | Defines operating models and CMPs, runs simulations, and calculates performance |
| `jmMSE26` | Reviewed specifications, selection evidence, and application instructions |

## Separation of responsibilities

`jmMSE26` is the documentation and application guide; it is not a separate MSE
model. Any calculation examples here must point to an exact saved version of
`jmMSE`. Operating models, recruitment and movement assumptions, reference
points, and performance measures remain defined in their scientific source
repositories.

## Reproducibility chain

Every performance claim should identify the operating models, CMP version,
number of simulation replicates, treatment of random variation, projection
period, performance-measure definition, saved results, and exact analysis
version. Every annual application should identify the data revision used and
the adopted CMP version.
